mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is particularly useful for serving large language models efficiently in production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d CLI tool, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the latest CLI binary and makes it executable.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create the Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running this deployment may incur significant costs based on the resources used.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. After upgrading, verify that the deployment is functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get all -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/upgrade.yaml

kubectl get pods

```

> Backup current configurations.
> This command applies the upgrade configuration.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure you have network access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the resource limits in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify that the service is up and check the service configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready' when you run 'kubectl get nodes'.

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
